title: bromus mollis var. neglectus
intro: Bromus mollis var. neglectus, also known as soft brome or meadow brome, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which may provide health benefits including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Soft brome can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various forms of administration, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
